items.question: What is the FIRST step in treating a cut?
If blood soaks through a bandage, what should you do?
Which of the following is BEST for cleaning a minor cut?
When should you seek medical attention for a cut?
What is a sign of infection?
When should you seek medical attention for a cut?
answers: The cut is deep or won't stop bleeding
It was caused by something rusty or dirty

items.question: When should you seek medical care for burns?
answers: The burn affects the face or respiratory tract
The victim has a third degree burn
The burn affects more than one body part or more than 15% of the body

items.question: How should you treat mild burns?
answers: Flush the area with large amounts of cool water
Apply butter or grease to soothe the skin
Rub ice directly on the burn
Cover it tightly with a wool cloth
items.correctAnswer: Flush the area with large amounts of cool water

items.question: What should you do after cooling and cleaning a burn?
answers: Apply a non-stick dressing to the burn site
Wrap it tightly with duct tape
Leave it open and rub it with a towel
Apply toothpaste to seal the area
items.correctAnswer: Apply a non-stick dressing to the burn site

items.question: How should you treat blisters on a burn?
answers: Do not break the blisters and wrap them in a sterile non-stick dressing
Pop the blisters with a sterilized needle
Squeeze the blisters to drain the fluid
Peel away the loose skin around the blister
items.correctAnswer: Do not break the blisters and wrap them in a sterile non-stick dressing

items.question: What are the signs of a 1st degree burn?
answers: Only the top layer of skin is affected
Redness, dry skin, or skin peeling

items.question: What are the signs of a second degree burn?
answers: The burn and skin look wet, shiny, or moist
There is significant swelling around the burned area

items.question: What are the signs of a third degree burn?
answers: Dry and charred skin, the area feeling very painful or not at all, and significant swelling
Not very painful, skin is red and shiny or moist
items.correctAnswer: Dry and charred skin, the area feeling very painful or not at all, and significant swelling

items.question: What are the care steps for a severe burn?
answers: Call 911 immediately and do not remove clothing stuck to the burn
Apply cool, not cold, water for five minutes then gently dry
If you can, elevate the burn above the heart and cover with a dressing
All of the above, in this order
items.correctAnswer: All of the above, in this order

items.question: What are signs of complete choking?
answers: Being unable to cough, speak, or breathe
Skin that becomes bluish or paler than normal

items.question: How can you reduce the risk of choking?
answers: Avoid talking and physical activity while chewing

items.question: How can you tell if someone is partially choking but not completely choking?
answers: Both hands are around their throat
They are coughing, wheezing, or gasping
They have a look of panic
items.correctAnswer: They are coughing, wheezing, or gasping

items.question: Why should someone who has suffered complete choking always seek medical attention?
answers: The lodged object could have damaged the airway
Back blows or abdominal thrusts could have caused damage

items.question: What should you do when a choking person becomes unresponsive?
answers: Pour water on their face to wake them
Sit them upright in a chair and wait
Continue back blows while they are on the ground

items.question: What is the recommended technique for helping a conscious adult who is completely choking?
answers: Alternating sets of 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts
items.correctAnswer: Alternating sets of 5 back blows and 5 abdominal thrusts

items.question: What should you do FIRST if you see someone who appears to be choking but is coughing forcefully?
answers: Lay them flat on their back

items.question: What body parts are MOST at risk for frostbite?
answers: Fingers, toes, ears, nose, and cheeks
items.correctAnswer: Fingers, toes, ears, nose, and cheeks

items.question: Which of the following is an early sign of frostbite?
answers: Skin that is warm and sweating
Skin that is red, cold, and tingling or numb
Bright pink skin with no other symptoms
Skin that is itchy and dry
items.correctAnswer: Skin that is red, cold, and tingling or numb

items.question: What should you do FIRST when treating frostbite?
answers: Rub the area vigorously to warm it
Get the person to a warm place and out of the cold
Apply ice to numb the pain
Break any blisters that have formed
items.correctAnswer: Get the person to a warm place and out of the cold

items.question: How should you rewarm a frostbitten body part?
answers: Soak it in hot water above 110°F
Hold it directly over an open flame
Soak it in warm (not hot) water around 100-105°F
items.correctAnswer: Soak it in warm (not hot) water around 100-105°F

items.question: Why should you NEVER rub or massage frostbitten skin?
answers: It can cause further damage to the tissue
It will make the area sweat
It will speed up rewarming too quickly
items.correctAnswer: It can cause further damage to the tissue

items.question: What should you do if there is a chance the frostbitten area might refreeze before reaching medical care?
answers: Apply ice to keep it numb

items.question: When should you seek emergency care for frostbite?
answers: When the skin is hard, white, or waxy
When blisters or blackened skin appear
When numbness does not go away after rewarming

items.question: What is hypothermia?
answers: An allergic skin reaction to cold
A type of frostbite affecting the fingers only

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of hypothermia?
What is the FIRST step in helping someone with hypothermia?
answers: Give them a hot alcoholic drink
Move them to a warm, dry place
Have them run in place to warm up
Put them in a hot bath right away
items.correctAnswer: Move them to a warm, dry place

items.question: What should you do with wet clothing on a hypothermia victim?
answers: Remove it and replace with dry clothing or blankets
Add more wet layers on top
Cut holes in it for ventilation
items.correctAnswer: Remove it and replace with dry clothing or blankets

items.question: Why should you NOT give an alcoholic drink to a hypothermia victim?
answers: Alcohol causes the body to lose heat faster
Alcohol tastes bad in the cold
items.correctAnswer: Alcohol causes the body to lose heat faster

items.question: If the victim is conscious and able to swallow, what is best to give them?
answers: A warm, sweet, non-alcoholic, non-caffeinated drink
items.correctAnswer: A warm, sweet, non-alcoholic, non-caffeinated drink

items.question: When should you call 911 for hypothermia?
answers: If the victim is unconscious or not breathing normally
If they are confused or unable to stop shivering
If symptoms do not improve with warming

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of heat exhaustion?
answers: Heavy sweating and pale, cool, clammy skin
Hot, dry skin with no sweating
Bright red rash on the chest
items.correctAnswer: Heavy sweating and pale, cool, clammy skin

items.question: What should you do FIRST when someone shows signs of heat exhaustion?
answers: Move them to a cool, shaded place
Have them keep exercising to sweat it out
items.correctAnswer: Move them to a cool, shaded place

items.question: What is the BEST thing to give a conscious heat exhaustion victim to drink?
answers: Cool water or a sports drink
items.correctAnswer: Cool water or a sports drink

items.question: How can you help cool someone with heat exhaustion?
answers: Loosen tight clothing and apply cool, wet cloths
Spray or sponge them with cool water

items.question: Which of the following are common symptoms of heat exhaustion?
When should heat exhaustion be treated as an emergency?
answers: When symptoms last longer than 1 hour or get worse
If the person becomes confused or loses consciousness
If they cannot keep fluids down

items.question: What is heat stroke?
answers: A mild reaction to warm weather
A life-threatening condition where the body cannot cool itself
items.correctAnswer: A life-threatening condition where the body cannot cool itself

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of heat stroke?
answers: Hot, red skin that may be dry or moist
Confusion, agitation, or loss of consciousness

items.question: What is the FIRST thing you should do if you suspect heat stroke?
answers: Wait to see if they get better
Have them sit in a hot tub

items.question: While waiting for emergency services, how should you cool a heat stroke victim?
answers: Move them to a cool place and apply cold, wet cloths or ice packs to neck, armpits, and groin
Have them run to keep their heart rate up
items.correctAnswer: Move them to a cool place and apply cold, wet cloths or ice packs to neck, armpits, and groin

items.question: Should you give water to an unconscious heat stroke victim?
answers: Yes, pour it down their throat
No, never give fluids to an unconscious person
items.correctAnswer: No, never give fluids to an unconscious person

items.question: What is the main difference between heat exhaustion and heat stroke?
answers: Heat stroke is more dangerous and often involves altered mental state
Heat exhaustion only happens at night
items.correctAnswer: Heat stroke is more dangerous and often involves altered mental state

items.question: What does the F in F.A.S.T. stand for when checking for a stroke?
What does the A in F.A.S.T. stand for?
What does the S in F.A.S.T. stand for?
What does the T in F.A.S.T. stand for?
What should you do if you suspect someone is having a stroke?
answers: Give them food and water immediately
Call 911 right away and note the time symptoms began
Have them lie down and sleep it off
Drive them yourself only if it's quicker
items.correctAnswer: Call 911 right away and note the time symptoms began

items.question: Why is it important to note the time stroke symptoms began?
answers: Treatment options may depend on how long ago symptoms started
It helps decide if you should sleep
It is needed for billing only
items.correctAnswer: Treatment options may depend on how long ago symptoms started

items.question: Which of the following is also a sign of a stroke?
answers: Sudden severe headache with no known cause
Sudden trouble seeing in one or both eyes
Sudden trouble walking, dizziness, or loss of balance

items.question: What should you do FIRST when someone is having a seizure?
answers: Hold them down to stop the shaking
Move objects away from them and protect their head
items.correctAnswer: Move objects away from them and protect their head

items.question: Should you put something in the mouth of someone having a seizure?
answers: Yes, a wallet to bite on
Yes, a spoon to hold the tongue
No, never put anything in their mouth
items.correctAnswer: No, never put anything in their mouth

items.question: After the seizure has stopped, how should you position the person if they are unresponsive but breathing?
answers: Flat on their back with pillows under their head
On their side in the recovery position
Standing up to walk it off
items.correctAnswer: On their side in the recovery position

items.question: When should you call 911 for a seizure?
answers: If the seizure lasts longer than 5 minutes
If another seizure happens right after
If the person is injured, pregnant, or has no history of seizures

items.question: Should you try to restrain someone who is having a seizure?
answers: Yes, hold them tightly until it stops
No, never try to restrain their movements
Only if they are an adult
Only if a doctor tells you to
items.correctAnswer: No, never try to restrain their movements

items.question: What should you do after a seizure ends?
answers: Stay with the person, reassure them, and let them rest
Wake them up with cold water
items.correctAnswer: Stay with the person, reassure them, and let them rest

items.question: What is a sign that someone is about to have a seizure or is currently having one?
answers: Sudden loss of awareness or staring blankly
Uncontrollable jerking of arms and legs

items.question: What should you do FIRST before starting CPR?
answers: Check the scene for safety, then check responsiveness and call 911
items.correctAnswer: Check the scene for safety, then check responsiveness and call 911

items.question: Where should you place your hands for chest compressions on an adult?
answers: On the center of the chest, on the lower half of the breastbone
On the left side of the chest only
items.correctAnswer: On the center of the chest, on the lower half of the breastbone

items.question: How deep should chest compressions be on an adult?
What is the correct rate for chest compressions?
answers: As fast as you can possibly go

items.question: What is the standard CPR ratio of compressions to rescue breaths for an adult?
If you are untrained or unwilling to give rescue breaths, what should you do?
answers: Do nothing and wait for help

items.question: When should you stop performing CPR?
answers: When the person starts to breathe normally on their own
When trained help arrives and takes over
When you are too exhausted to continue

items.question: What is an AED?
answers: A device that measures blood pressure
An automated external defibrillator that can shock the heart back into rhythm
items.correctAnswer: An automated external defibrillator that can shock the heart back into rhythm

items.question: What should you do FIRST after a bee sting?
answers: Squeeze the stinger out with tweezers
Scrape the stinger out with a flat-edged object like a credit card
Suck the venom out with your mouth
items.correctAnswer: Scrape the stinger out with a flat-edged object like a credit card

items.question: After removing a stinger, what should you do?
answers: Wash the area with soap and water
Apply ice or a cold pack to reduce swelling
Watch for signs of an allergic reaction

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of a SEVERE allergic reaction to an insect bite or sting?
answers: Swelling of the face, lips, or tongue

items.question: What should you do if someone has a severe allergic reaction to a sting?
answers: Tell them to walk it off
Call 911 immediately and help them use an epinephrine auto-injector if they have one
Apply heat to the sting site
items.correctAnswer: Call 911 immediately and help them use an epinephrine auto-injector if they have one

items.question: How should you remove a tick that is attached to the skin?
answers: Use fine-tipped tweezers to grasp it close to the skin and pull straight up steadily
Cover it in petroleum jelly to suffocate it
Pinch it off with your fingers and twist
items.correctAnswer: Use fine-tipped tweezers to grasp it close to the skin and pull straight up steadily

items.question: After removing a tick, what should you do?
answers: Clean the bite area with soap and water or rubbing alcohol
Watch for a rash or flu-like symptoms in the following weeks
Save the tick in a sealed container in case identification is needed

items.question: Which of the following can help relieve itching from a typical bug bite?
What is shock?
answers: A life-threatening condition where not enough blood and oxygen reach the body's organs
items.correctAnswer: A life-threatening condition where not enough blood and oxygen reach the body's organs

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of shock?
What is the FIRST thing you should do if you suspect shock?
answers: Have them stand up and walk
Splash cold water on their face

items.question: How should you position a conscious person who may be in shock (and has no suspected spinal injury)?
answers: Curled into a ball on their side

items.question: Should you give food or water to a person in shock?
answers: Yes, give them a big meal
No, do not give food or drink
items.correctAnswer: No, do not give food or drink

items.question: What else can help someone who is in shock while waiting for help?
answers: Keep them warm with a blanket
Reassure them and keep them calm

items.question: Which of the following can cause shock?
Which of the following is an early sign of dehydration?
What is the BEST drink for treating mild dehydration?
answers: Water or an oral rehydration drink
items.correctAnswer: Water or an oral rehydration drink

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of SEVERE dehydration?
When should you seek medical care for dehydration?
answers: If symptoms are severe or the person is very young, elderly, or ill
If they cannot keep fluids down
If they show signs of confusion or fainting

items.question: How can you help prevent dehydration in hot weather?
answers: Drink water regularly even before feeling thirsty
Take breaks in the shade or in air conditioning
Avoid drinks high in alcohol or caffeine

items.question: Why are children and elderly people at higher risk of dehydration?
answers: They lose fluids more easily and may not recognize or communicate thirst as well
They drink more water than other people
They sweat less than anyone else
They never need to drink water
items.correctAnswer: They lose fluids more easily and may not recognize or communicate thirst as well

items.question: What position is SAFEST for someone who is vomiting and may be drowsy or unresponsive?
answers: On their side in the recovery position
Sitting upright in a tight seatbelt
items.correctAnswer: On their side in the recovery position

items.question: After someone has stopped vomiting, what should you give them first?
answers: Small sips of water or an oral rehydration drink
items.correctAnswer: Small sips of water or an oral rehydration drink

items.question: Which of the following is a sign that vomiting may be a medical emergency?
answers: Vomit that contains blood or looks like coffee grounds
Severe abdominal pain or a stiff neck
Signs of severe dehydration or confusion

items.question: If a child is vomiting, when should you call a doctor?
answers: If vomiting lasts more than a day or the child cannot keep fluids down
If they show signs of dehydration
If there is blood in the vomit or they have a high fever

items.question: What should you do AFTER someone vomits?
answers: Help them rinse their mouth with water
Let them rest and avoid solid food at first

items.question: Should you give an unconscious person something to drink after they have vomited?
answers: Yes, pour water in their mouth
No, never give fluids to an unconscious person
items.correctAnswer: No, never give fluids to an unconscious person

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of a broken bone?
answers: Inability to move or bear weight on the area

items.question: What should you do FIRST if someone may have a broken bone?
answers: Try to push the bone back into place
Keep the injured area still and call for help if needed
Have them walk on it to test it
Massage the area to reduce swelling
items.correctAnswer: Keep the injured area still and call for help if needed

items.question: Should you try to straighten a broken bone yourself?
answers: No, do not try to realign or push a broken bone back in place
Only if it is in the arm
items.correctAnswer: No, do not try to realign or push a broken bone back in place

items.question: How can you help reduce swelling and pain from a possible fracture?
answers: Apply a cold pack wrapped in a thin cloth
Heat the area with a hot water bottle
Soak the limb in hot water
items.correctAnswer: Apply a cold pack wrapped in a thin cloth

items.question: What is an OPEN fracture?
answers: A bone that is bent but not broken
A break where the bone breaks through the skin or a wound exposes the bone
A small crack in the bone
items.correctAnswer: A break where the bone breaks through the skin or a wound exposes the bone

items.question: What should you do for an open fracture?
answers: Call 911, control bleeding gently around the wound, and keep the area still
Wash the bone with hot water
Wrap it tightly to push the bone back in
items.correctAnswer: Call 911, control bleeding gently around the wound, and keep the area still

items.question: When should you call 911 for a possible broken bone?
answers: When the bone is sticking out of the skin
When the injury is to the head, neck, back, hip, pelvis, or thigh
When the person cannot move the limb or is in severe pain

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of a SEVERE allergic reaction (anaphylaxis)?
answers: Swelling of the face, lips, or tongue
Sudden drop in blood pressure or fainting

items.question: What is the FIRST thing you should do if someone is having a severe allergic reaction?
answers: Wait to see if they get better

items.question: What is an epinephrine auto-injector (such as an EpiPen) used for?
Where is an epinephrine auto-injector typically given?
answers: Into a vein in the arm
On the skin of the face

items.question: After using an epinephrine auto-injector, what should you do next?
answers: Call 911 (if not already called) because the person still needs medical care
Have them eat a big meal
Give them another injection right away no matter what
items.correctAnswer: Call 911 (if not already called) because the person still needs medical care

items.question: Which of the following can trigger a severe allergic reaction?
answers: Foods like peanuts, tree nuts, or shellfish

items.question: Which of the following is a sign of a MILD allergic reaction?
answers: Itchy skin or a small rash
Mild sneezing or a runny nose
